chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-2133 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 09.10.2019 Mr. Ali Ahmed Palh, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, A.A.G. alongwith Salamat Ali Memon, Additional Deputy Commissioner-I, Mirpurkhas, Ghulam Mustafa Yousfani, Mukhtiarkar, Sindhry on behalf of Deputy Commissioner, Mirpurkhas and DSP (Legal) Ahmed Karim Jilani on behalf of SSP, Mirpurkhas. -.-.-. Learned Counsel for the petitioner has filed a statement along with certain case law, which is taken on record. Learned Additional Advocate General Sindh has also filed a statement along with comments of respondents No.3 and 5, and has supplied copies thereof to the learned Counsel for the petitioner. After arguing at some length, learned Additional Advocate General Sindh and officials present have submitted that if the detainee namely Wajid Ali Laghari is ready to give an undertaking that no law and order situation would be created by him in future and he would not misuse social media against government officials, they have no objection to setting aside of the impugned order, whereby the detention of the detainee has been extended for further sixty days. Mr. Ali Ahmed Palh learned counsel for the petitioner has given undertaking on behalf of the detainee that he except peaceful protest if need arises, which is his constitutional right, will not create any law and order situation, will not threaten government officials and will not misuse the social media against the District Management including SSP Mirpurkhas in future. He further submits that no such action was done by him in the past either.

Ali Ahmed Palh learned counsel for the petitioner has given undertaking on behalf of the detainee that he except peaceful protest if need arises, which is his constitutional right, will not create any law and order situation, will not threaten government officials and will not misuse the social media against the District Management including SSP Mirpurkhas in future. He further submits that no such action was done by him in the past either. This undertaking of Mr. Ali Ahmed Palh, Advocate, is made part of this order and the impugned order dated 26.09.2019, in view of above, is set aside. The detainee, who is confined in Central Prison, Mirpurkhas, shall be released forthwith if not required in any other custody case. This petition is disposed of in the terms as stated above. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
